content.full_text:
Law Office of Glen H. Spain
Counselor and Attorney at Law

Phone 937-2324                                                                P.O. Box 205
Area Code 707                                                          Comptche, CA 95427


                                                                        March 10, 1992

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Legal and Technical Assistance Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
SACRAMENTO, CA  95814

     RE: Request for Advice Letter pursuant to Gov. C. 83114

To the Examiner:

     This is a formal request for an advice letter pursuant to the
provisions of Gov. C. 83114(b), based on the following fact situations:

     Relevant facts: I am a proponent of two statewide ballot initiatives.
One recipient committee is incorporated, and the other committee is
organized as a project within a corporation which also has other unrelated
activities.  Both are nonprofit public benefit corporations.  I serve as
general legal counsel for both committees, whose purpose is to qualify
those ballot measures for the statewide ballot.  I serve at the pleasure of
the Board of Directors of each organization, and if the Board chooses to do
so it can fire me and hire any other attorney at any time.  My role as
General Counsel is purely advisory.  I am neither an officer nor member of
the Board of Directors of either corporation, and I have no authority to
handle any of the funds of those committees.  I am compensated as an
independent professional only, and not as an employee.  Only the Boards of
Directors establish policy for their respective campaigns, hire employees
and manage the affairs of their committees.  I have no other role with
respect to these committee other than as a legal advisor.  I remain,
however, an official proponent of both measures.

     QUESTION 1:  Should I now be classed as a person who controls a
committee?  Should the committee be classed as a "controlled committee"
pursuant to Gov. C. 82016?

     QUESTION 2: If I were to become a member of the Board of Directors of
either committee, would I then be classed as a person who controls a
committee?  Would the committee then be classed as a "controlled committee"
pursuant to Gov. C. 82016 solely as a result of my change of status with
regard to the governing body of those committees?

     QUESTION 3:  One of these committees is already classed as a
"sponsored committee" of one of the corporations under Gov. C. 82048.7(4),
in that the corporation (through its Board of Directors) sets the policies
for soliciting contributions or making expenditures of committee funds.

                                        -1-

I can see this is a mostly blank page with very minimal visible content. There appear to be only a few faint marks or dots in the upper right area of the page, but they are not legible enough to transcribe as specific text.

[illegible]

FPPC Advice Request
March 10, 1992

That sponsoring organization is a pre-existing nonprofit corporation with
other activities in addition to the activities of the recipient committee,
and so classification as a "sponsored committee" seemed the most
appropriate classification under the code. If I, as a proponent, were to
also become a member of the Board of Directors of that organization, would
that also make the committee a "controlled committee" under Gov. C. 82016?

Please provide your advice based on the above information within 21
working days. This information will be needed for my guidance in filing
the next disclosure statements due.

Thank you for your help in clarifying this situation.

SINCERELY,

GLEN H. SPAIN
ATTORNEY AT LAW

GHS/lt

-2-
